channel.title: Rosa Luxemburg Foundation – English
channel.description: Latest news and publications from the Rosa Luxemburg Foundation (English)

item.title: Britain's Greens Are on the Rise
item.description: Zack Polanski's Greens are skyrocketing in the polls, but can they out-flank Labour on the left?

item.title: Belfast Race Riots: How Loyalism Turned on Migrants
item.description: Northern Ireland's summer riots reveal how old sectarian conflicts created the conditions for new pogroms.

item.title: Happy Birthday, USA?
item.description: As the USA celebrates it's 250th independence day this July, a Black perspective on centuries of injustice.

item.title: Kyrgyzstan's Gig Economy: Taxed But Unprotected
item.description: Platform work is booming in Kyrgyzstan, but labour law hasn't caught up with the gig economy.

item.title: "Resilience Doesn't Mean Being Strong All the Time"
item.description: Theatre director Wisam Al-Deirawi on the psychological burden facing women in Gaza and how art becomes a tool for healing

item.title: “Many voters want a more left-leaning government”
item.description: Danish socialist party Enhedslisten's Pelle Dragsted explains how the left won concessions from Denmark's new government coalition

item.title: Colombia’s Trump
item.description: Colombia elects right-winger Abelardo de la Espriella, continuing the right's ascendance in Latin America

item.title: Congo's Minerals Power the World. Why Are Its People Going Hungry?
item.description: The DRC supplies cobalt for EV batteries, smartphones, and the green transition. But little remains for the local economy.

item.title: China: The Loneliness of Gen Z
item.description: Chinese economic growth has opened up new opportunities for millions of people—but at the same time has undermined the social structures that fostered a sense of belonging within organized

item.title: Gaza’s Food Sovereignty Crisis
item.description: In Gaza, the hungry forage for food while farmers and seed keepers struggle to preserve Palestinian agriculture
